items.title: Landslides kill 15 in Philippines as biggest typhoon in decades nears Taiwan
items.summary: KEELUNG, Taiwan: Fifteen people were killed in landslides in the Philippines, and more than 2,000 in Taiwan were evacuated from their homes, as the biggest typhoon in decades neared the region on Friday. Typhoon Bavi is expected to pound Taiwan's north and east, as well as Japan's remote southwestern islands, on Friday and Saturday before smashing into China, which has been hit by deadly storms this week. Keelung grocery store owner Chang Shih-huo, 76, told AFP.

items.title: Johor polls: 2.7 mil voters ready to decide state's future
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: More than 2.7 million registered voters, including a significant number of first-time voters, will determine Johor's future as preparations for the 16th Johor election enter the final stage. The Election Commission (EC) has intensified its final checks on ballot boxes and election equipment ahead of polling day, with EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun leading inspection visits at several locations in Pontian and Kukup. The inspections are part of the EC's final preparations to ensure all election materials, including ballot boxes, are ready for use across Johor's 56 state constituencies.

items.title: Anwar makes final appeal for Johoreans to choose capable leadership
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Johoreans to make a decisive choice at the state election tomorrow, saying their votes will determine the next chapter of the state's future. "Tomorrow is a historic day that will determine the next chapter in the story of Johor's future and its people," he said in a final appeal ahead of polling day. Anwar said the government had also succeeded in restoring Malaysia's international standing and confidence among global investors.

items.title: Ringgit ends firmer as safe haven demand for US dollar fades
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended the week on a stronger note against the US dollar on Friday, as safe-haven demand for the greenback faded. At 6pm, the local currency climbed to 4.0695/0745 against the US dollar from 4.0750/0795 at Thursday's close. AmBank Group FX strategist Yim Soon Kah told Bernama that the weaker US dollar was driven by reports that US and Iranian mediators would continue peace negotiations despite the recent military escalation, reducing demand for the greenback as a safe-haven currency and for higher-yielding US Treasuries.

items.title: OPEC+ countries produced oil 7.5mil bpd below target in June – IEA
items.summary: MOSCOW: OPEC+ countries with voluntary restrictions increased oil production in June by 2.1 million barrels per day to 32.4 million barrels, and produced 7.5 million barrels per day below the quota level, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said in its fresh report on Friday, Sputnik/RIA Novosti reported. According to report, the total OPEC+ oil production in June amounted to 32.44 million barrels per day against 30.3 million barrels in May. Thus, OPEC+ countries produced 7.51 million barrels less than the target.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 10, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher, buoyed by a strong rebound in banking stocks that lifted the broader market, in line with gains across regional bourses and Wall Street. Investor sentiment improved as concerns over escalating geopolitical tensions eased, with oil prices stabilising toward the end of a volatile week. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Two men with criminal records detained over Petaling Jaya hotel murder probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — Police have arrested two local men to assist in the investigation of the murder of a man who was found dead at a hotel in Sunway Mentari, Petaling Jaya, last Wednesday. Petaling Jaya District Police Chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said that both suspects, in their 20s and 30s, were detained following initial information received by the police at 11:23 pm regarding an unconscious local man at the hotel. “Upon arrival at the scene, police found the victim, in his 30s, lifeless.

items.title: Man nearly sucked out of 'detached' window on Ryanair flight
items.summary: THESSALONIKI, Greece: A man was nearly sucked out the window of a Ryanair flight when it "detached" mid-air en route to Germany, with other passengers pulling him back inside, witnesses and officials said Friday. The passenger, described as a tourist from Serbia on a flight from Thessaloniki in Greece to Memmingen in Germany, has been hospitalised with friction burns but was otherwise in good condition, authorities said. Ryanair in a statement said the flight "returned to Thessaloniki shortly after takeoff when a passenger window detached during the flight.

items.title: Fahmi heads Penang Governor’s honours list, with Ancom Nylex CEO among recipients
items.summary: Fahmi will be conferred the Darjah Panglima Pangkuan Negeri, which carries the title “Dato’ Seri”, the Penang state government said in a statement today. Another 19 recipients will receive the Darjah Gemilang Pangkuan Negeri, which also carries the “Dato’ Seri” title. A further 72 recipients will receive the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri, which also carries the “Dato’” title.

items.title: Advancecon lands RM121.7mil Port Dickson job
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Advancecon Holdings Bhd has bagged a RM121.66 million subcontract to undertake earthworks and associated works for an off-river storage (ORS) facility supporting a data centre water supply development in Port Dickson, Negri Sembilan. The subcontract covers the construction and completion of earthworks and related works for the ORS under the proposed water supply scheme development with a production capacity of 65 million litres per day for a data centre at Linggi, Port Dickson. This includes the PKNS Cyber Valley development, the raw water pipeline contract for the Rasau water treatment plant and finishing works for the MRT Line 2 project in Cyberjaya.

items.title: US-Iran escalation could threaten 2027 oil market surplus – IEA
items.summary: The IEA said global oil supply rose by 4.1 million bpd in June, but remained 9.4 million bpd below pre-war levels. LASTING PEACE DEAL NEEDED FOR NORMALISATION The agency predicts supply will expand by 7.5 million bpd next year after a 3.7 million bpd contraction this year, but that is contingent on improved Hormuz transits. The Paris-based agency, which advises industrialised nations, sees global oil demand falling by one million bpd this year, before rebounding to rise two million bpd in 2027.

items.title: Blogger fined RM11,000 over offensive TikTok post targeting Ismail Sabri, Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The Sessions Court here fined blogger Jufazli Shi Ahmad RM11,000 today after finding him guilty of transmitting offensive communications against former Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ismail Sabri Yaakob and UMNO four years ago. “After evaluating the entire defence and the witnesses’ testimonies, the court finds Jufazli guilty and convicts him as charged. Jufazli paid the fine today.

items.title: SoftBank, PayPay in talks to invest in Seven & i
items.summary: TOKYO: SoftBank Corp and mobile payments operator PayPay are in talks to invest in retail giant Seven & i Holdings, Bloomberg News reported on Friday. The investment will likely total several hundred billion yen and Sumitomo Mitsui Card, a unit of Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, may also take a stake, Bloomberg said. SoftBank Corp, the domestic telecommunications arm of SoftBank Group, has been developing AI tools for enterprise clients with ChatGPT-maker OpenAI.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia closes higher on rebound in financial stocks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher on Friday, supported by a strong rebound in financial services stocks that lifted the overall market sentiment, in line with gains across regional markets. At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 13.85 points, or 0.83 per cent, to 1,691.49 from Thursday's close of 1,677.64. Turnover increased to 2.85 billion units valued at RM2.25 billion from 2.64 billion units valued at RM2.19 billion on Wednesday.

items.title: Fire dept readies 667 assets to take on Super El Nino phenomenon
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Fire and Rescue Department has deployed 667 assets nationwide in preparation for the potential impact of the Super El Nino phenomenon, which is expected to affect Malaysia from early next year. Its director-general, Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad, said all state directors have been instructed to strategically position assets in high-risk areas, particularly in the northern states of Peninsular Malaysia and Sabah's east coast. "The department has made the necessary preparations, and I have personally met with zone chiefs across the country to review their early preparations before we begin facing the impact of Super El Nino from January to April next year.

items.title: Johor election: Johoreans in Singapore return home to cast ballots
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A day before polling day for the Johor election, Johoreans working in Singapore have begun making their way home to cast their ballots. Many are willing to endure long journeys to fulfil their responsibility as voters. She said demand for bus tickets had also increased as many people took the opportunity to return home earlier.

items.title: Fire department prepares 667 assets as Super El Nino raises fire risk from January
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 10 — The Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department has deployed 667 assets nationwide in preparation for the potential impact of the Super El Nino phenomenon, which is expected to affect Malaysia from early next year. Its director-general, Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said all state directors have been instructed to strategically position assets in high-risk areas, particularly in the northern states of Peninsular Malaysia and Sabah’s east coast. “The department has made the necessary preparations, and I have personally met with zone chiefs across the country to review their early preparations before we begin facing the impact of Super El Niño from January to April next year.

items.title: Perkeso's Lindung 24 covers 2.27mil workers, collects RM58mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) has collected nearly RM58 million contributions under the Non-Employment Injury scheme (Lindung 24 Jam) since its implementation, with more than 2.27 million workers now covered under it. Perkeso group chief executive Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said as of July 9, a total of 207,000 employers had registered for the scheme, covering 1.92 million Malaysian workers and 307,000 foreign workers. Responding to concerns over delays in claims, Mohammed Azman said Perkeso actively assists claimants instead of waiting for them to come forward.

items.title: Malaysia-Thai prawn, sea bass issues to be resolved through science
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia and Thailand are in talks to address concerns surrounding the trade of sea bass and prawns, with both countries working towards an amicable resolution. Fisheries director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said the discussions focused on enhancing technical requirements to facilitate trade without compromising biosecurity, food safety and aquatic animal health. Adnan said Malaysia had submitted 35 technical questions concerning the prawn trade to seek further clarification following an initial assessment of the information provided by Thailand, before finalising any risk assessment.

items.title: Couple linked to Ah Long activities charged over petrol bomb attacks in Ipoh
items.summary: IPOH, July 10 — A married couple pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to three charges of mischief, including throwing a petrol bomb (molotov cocktail) causing property damage early last month. M. Dhinakaran and B. Rahtheneswary, both 34 years old, made the plea after all charges were read out in Tamil before Judge Ainul Shahrin Mohamad. Both charges were brought under Section 436 of the Penal Code read together with Section 34 of the same code, which provides for a maximum jail term of 20 years and a fine, if convicted.

items.title: Kelantan police boost security after graffiti threats, suspicious bag found at shooting range
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 10 — Police have tightened security measures at all police facilities in Kelantan following a series of threats at the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) Kelantan Contingent Shooting Range in Bachok, including deploying security personnel and requesting the installation of closed-circuit television (CCTV) systems. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the measures were taken after acts of vandalism involving graffiti depicting threats were found on the fence of the PDRM Kelantan Contingent Shooting Range, along with the discovery of a black bag in the area believed to be linked to an attempt to intimidate the police. He said that following the incidents, he instructed the Bachok district police chief to station personnel at the Bachok Shooting Range, while also requesting CCTVs to be installed at all police premises to strengthen security and assist investigations.

items.title: Investigation into alleged petroleum cargo document discrepancies at Tanjung Langsat Port still ongoing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The investigation into alleged discrepancies involving petroleum cargo documents at Johor’s Tanjung Langsat Port is still ongoing, according to Maritime Network Sdn Bhd chief executive Datuk Seri R. Jeyenderan. Jeyenderan said he was satisfied with the response received from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) via email in mid-June, which informed him that the case had been received and reviewed by the Information Assessment Committee (JMM), and that investigations were still underway. “We respect the MACC and the investigation process.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines cancels Kuala Lumpur-Taipei flights due to Typhoon Bavi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines has cancelled its flights between Kuala Lumpur and Taipei scheduled for July 11 due to adverse weather conditions associated with Typhoon Bavi. In a travel advisory issued today, the airline said the affected flights were MH366 (Kuala Lumpur-Taipei) and MH367 (Taipei-Kuala Lumpur). Further updates regarding flight operations will be communicated directly to affected passengers," it said.

items.title: Tomorrow decides Johor’s future, Anwar calls: Return home and vote for fairness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on Johoreans, including those living outside the state, to return and cast their ballots in tomorrow’s state election, describing it as a pivotal moment for Johor’s future. In a Facebook post, Anwar said the election would determine the next chapter in Johor’s story and urged voters to choose leaders who are honest, capable and committed to serving the people. “With humility and respect, I ask you to place your trust in Pakatan Harapan to lead Johor with integrity, sound governance and fairness, ensuring equal opportunities for all,” Anwar wrote.

items.title: Fire Dept: Ammonia leak at Ipoh ice factory caused by unsecured cylinder; Hazmat team contains spill
items.summary: IPOH, July 10 — A 400-litre ammonia gas cylinder, which was believed to have not been properly closed, caused a gas leak and chemical spill at an ice manufacturing factory in the Menglembu Industrial Area here today. He said the leak was caused by the gas cylinder that was not properly closed, resulting in uncontrolled gas pressure release and a spillage of about seven litres of ammonia. Sabarodzi said two Hazmat members, fully equipped with personal protective equipment (PPE), then entered the risk area to close the valve on the gas cylinder, thereby stopping the gas release.

items.title: High Court upholds 12-year jail term for religious healer who raped teen
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A 58-year-old religious healer will remain behind bars for 12 years after the High Court dismissed his appeal against his conviction and sentence for raping a 17-year-old girl four years ago. Hazlina said the victim was a credible witness and the prosecution had successfully proved its case beyond reasonable doubt. "When the victim testified in court, approximately two years and six months after the incident, she was already 19 years old and employed.

items.title: RTM implements strict SOP to filter LGBT content across TV, radio and digital platforms, says Communications Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The Department of Broadcasting Malaysia (RTM) has implemented a strict standard operating procedure (SOP) to ensure all content produced, acquired, and broadcast on its television, radio, and digital platforms complies with established broadcasting guidelines. The Ministry of Communications said the SOP requires filtering lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) content in all local and foreign programmes to prevent broadcasting materials that may negatively affect society, especially children. The ministry added that all local and international content, including children’s animated products, on its television channels must undergo quality control (QC) by the Creative Content Unit (UKK).

items.title: Johor election: Bersama unlikely to win seats but could split votes
items.summary: KLUANG: Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) may not emerge as winners in the Johor election, but the party could upset the calculations of major coalitions by splitting votes in key battleground constituencies, political analysts said. While the party is unlikely to secure any seats in its maiden state election outing, analysts believe its presence could prove significant in marginal constituencies where victory margins are narrow and a small shift in support could determine the outcome. Universiti Teknologi Malaysia Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities associate professor Dr Mazlan Ali said Bersama's greatest impact would likely be felt in marginal seats where winning margins were below 1,000 votes.

items.title: Home Ministry outlines projects to improve security, efficiency and services in Johor, says Saifuddin Nasution
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — The Home Ministry (KDN) will continue to focus on implementing various development projects in Johor to strengthen security infrastructure, enhance operational efficiency and improve public service delivery in the state. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said Johor is an important state in the southern region of the country given its role as one of the nation’s key hubs for security, public services and international entry points. He said the development projects involve several agencies under the ministry, including the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Immigration Department, the National Registration Department (JPN), the Prisons Department, the National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) and the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA).

items.title: Firefighters battle Kuching ice cream factory blaze spreading to neighbouring premises, all workers safe
items.summary: KUCHING, July 10 — A fire broke out at an ice cream factory and spread to a neighbouring factory at Demak Laut Industrial Park here on Friday afternoon. Firefighters from the Tabuan Jaya and Petra Jaya fire stations are at the scene battling the blaze after receiving a distress call at 12.23pm. “Firefighters are at the scene to extinguish the fire,” the Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) said.

items.title: RM6.19mil frozen pork seized in Miri warehouse raid
items.summary: MIRI: Police arrested a local man and seized RM6.19 million worth of frozen pork products suspected of being imported without valid permits during an operation at a warehouse here last night. Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri said the operation reflects the police's commitment to strengthening enforcement against illegal food products and the storage of frozen food of unknown halal status. Inspections at the premises uncovered 30,000kg of cooked frozen pork large intestines, 18,000kg of pork loin packed in 15 pallets, 750kg of pork in one pallet, and several pallets of bone-in pork bellies and frozen pork shoulder.

items.title: Fahmi heads Penang honours list, late Nordin Ahmad receives Datukship
items.summary: He said a total of 1,221 individuals would receive state honours, stars and medals, comprising 101 recipients of state orders and another 1,120 recipients of stars and medals. He said 19 recipients would receive the Darjah Gemilang Pangkuan Negeri (DGPN), which also carries the title Datuk Seri. Among them are Mara Incorporated Sdn Bhd chairman Datuk Wira Abdul Halim Mohammad, InvestPenang chief executive officer Datuk Loo Lee Lian, Pelita Samudra Pertama (M) Sdn Bhd managing director Tan Sri Datuk Murugan Doraisamy, Malaya Chief Judge Datuk Seri Hashim Hamzah, Menara Rezeki Sdn Bhd managing director Datuk Saiful Nizam Mohd Yusoff and M Summit Group chairman Datuk Moh See Eng.

items.title: Johor polls: Give Pakatan a chance, Hannah Yeoh urges Pekan Nanas voters
items.summary: PONTIAN, July 10 — DAP deputy secretary-general Hannah Yeoh has called on voters in the Pekan Nanas state constituency to give Pakatan Harapan (PH) the opportunity to serve the area once again. “(PH candidate for the Pekan Nanas state seat) Yeo Tung Siong is an experienced candidate who understands the appropriate channels and agencies to engage in resolving the people’s issues. N55: Pekan Nanas — 37,556 voters 1.

items.title: EAIC finds procedural lapses in footballers' citizenship applications
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission (EAIC) has found procedural irregularities in the citizenship applications of seven naturalised footballers, including an unusually fast assessment process and lapses in immigration and citizenship requirements. "Among the irregularities identified were those involving the entry permit issuance process, including interview and security screening procedures conducted by the Immigration Department, as well as the department's security screening process and the Malay Language Knowledge Test administered by the NRD for citizenship approval. The commission said its investigation found that the Federal Constitution empowers the Home Minister to consider citizenship applications by naturalisation under Article 19.

items.title: Malaysia, Thailand in technical talks to address sea bass and prawn trade concerns, says Fisheries D-G
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 10 — Malaysia and Thailand are in talks to address concerns surrounding the trade of sea bass and prawns, with both countries working towards an amicable resolution. Fisheries director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said the discussions focused on enhancing technical requirements to facilitate trade without compromising biosecurity, food safety and aquatic animal health. Adnan said Malaysia had submitted 35 technical questions concerning the prawn trade to seek further clarification following an initial assessment of the information provided by Thailand, before finalising any risk assessment.

items.title: Johor DAP deputy chairman Sheikh Umar apologises for riding without helmet, says he will pay the fine
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, July 10 — Johor DAP deputy chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali has apologised after being spotted riding a motorcycle without a helmet during nomination day in Paloh. I apologise for riding a motorcycle without a helmet on nomination morning in Paloh. Loke also shared a photograph of Barisan Nasional campaigners, including a former minister, similarly riding without helmets.

items.title: Lawyer vows continuity in Mengkibol if elected
items.summary: With pix NSTP/Aizuddin Saad KLUANG: Pakatan Harapan candidate for Mengkibol, Chu Poh Yee, has pledged to continue the work of former assemblyman Chew Chong Sin if she wins the election. The lawyer, who is making her electoral debut, said many constituents had approached her during the campaign with issues they hoped she could resolve if elected. [[nid:1485338]] [[nid:1485337]] © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Anwar surprises nine-year-old Felda boy with bicycle
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today shared the heartwarming story of nine-year-old Muhammad Afif Ikhwan, who travelled through an oil palm plantation with his grandmother in the hope of meeting him. "What may appear to us as a small dream was, in fact, big enough to move his grandmother, Rosimah Mohammad, to ride a motorcycle through an oil palm plantation to fulfil her grandson's wish. Yesterday, Bernama reported that Rosimah, 58, from Kampung Pudu, Segamat, rode a motorcycle along oil palm plantation tracks solely to give her grandson the opportunity to see the Prime Minister up close.

items.title: Dengue cases up 35pc nationwide, 34 deaths recorded
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia recorded a 34.8 per cent surge in dengue cases this year, with 42,848 cases reported as of yesterday compared with 31,790 during the same period last year. Dengue-related deaths also jumped by 78.9 per cent to 34, up from 19 fatalities recorded during the corresponding period in 2025, the Health Ministry said in a statement today. Selangor recorded 19,313 cases, a 43.2 per cent increase from the same period last year, while Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya reported 7,909 cases, representing an 83.8 per cent spike.

items.title: MetMalaysia forecasts morning rain, thunderstorms in several Johor districts on polling day
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — Several areas in Johor are expected to experience rain and thunderstorms tomorrow, polling day for the 16th Johor state election, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). Based on the weather forecast published on the department’s official website today, rain is expected in the morning in Johor Bahru, Batu Pahat, Kulai, Muar, Pontian and Tangkak. However, no rain is forecast in the six districts in the afternoon and at night.

items.title: DAP fields Mugunthan for Seremban Jaya state seat
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Pakatan Harapan has named S. Mugunthan, a research officer to a Negri Sembilan executive councillor, as its candidate for the Seremban Jaya state seat in the upcoming state election on Aug 1. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke announced Mugunthan, 40, will replace four-term incumbent Datuk P. Gunasekaran, who is stepping down after representing the constituency since 2008. Loke expressed confidence that Mugunthan would retain the seat for DAP, noting the party has held the constituency since 2008 when it was known as the Senawang state seat before redelineation.

items.title: Johor polls: Anthony Loke hopes voter turnout will exceed 70pc, cites strong demand for buses and trains
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 10 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke hopes voter turnout in the Johor state election tomorrow will exceed 70 per cent to ensure that the government elected truly reflects the voice of the majority of the people. In the last state election, he said, voter turnout was around 54 per cent due to border closures that prevented Malaysians working in Singapore from returning home to vote. At the event, Loke also announced Negeri Sembilan state executive councillor J. Arul Kumar’s research officer, S. Mugunthan, 40, as the party’s new candidate for the Seremban Jaya state seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election.

items.title: Johor polls: PH strongholds at risk if Chinese voter turnout drops below 65%
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The turnout rate among Chinese voters could prove decisive in Pakatan Harapan's (PH) performance in the Johor election, with low participation threatening several of its traditional strongholds, according to Ilham Centre. The research body said Barisan Nasional (BN) and PH were contesting on different battlegrounds, with BN relying on stable Malay support while PH depended heavily on urban and Chinese-majority constituencies. Ilham Centre said BN had gained ground in Malay-majority areas following weakening confidence in Perikatan Nasional (PN) as an alternative political force.

items.title: #NSTviral: Rat in food display sparks outrage, calls for action [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video showing a rat roaming inside a restaurant's food display cabinet has sparked public outrage, with netizens calling for immediate action from the authorities. The video has raised significant concerns over food hygiene, as rats are known to carry diseases and contaminate food through droppings, urine, and fur. X user Shuaib Shahzaman claimed the area was already known for poor hygiene and urged Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) to intensify enforcement.

items.title: Johor polls enter final campaign stretch as 2.7 million voters prepare to elect 56 assemblymen tomorrow
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — After two weeks of intense campaigning, the curtain will fall on the 16th Johor state election campaign at 11.59 pm tonight, bringing to an end all canvassing activities, both on the ground and online. Overall voter turnout in the 2022 Johor state election stood at 54.92 per cent. Party flags are seen in Johor Bahru ahead of the 16th Johor state election on July 11.

items.title: Lindung 24 contributions in June may not be refunded
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Contributions already paid into the Social Security Organisation's (Perkeso) Lindung 24 Jam scheme before employees opt out are unlikely to be refunded. Perkeso said the payments made earlier remain valid under the law despite the government's decision to make participation voluntary. Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said the scheme had been implemented under the Employees' Social Security Act 1969 (Act 4), which took effect on June 1, meaning contributions made before an employee formally opts out remain legally valid.

items.title: EAIC report: Citizenship approvals for seven naturalised players processed too quickly, not orderly
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 10 — The Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission (EAIC) has found irregularities in the citizenship approval process for seven naturalised Malaysian footballers and has urged the Home Ministry and related agencies to tighten procedures and introduce clear guidelines. The commission said it received complaints over the approvals and subsequently set up a special task force under Section 17 of the Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission Act 2009 to investigate. The probe involved the National Registration Department (NRD), Immigration Department, Royal Malaysia Police and the Football Association of Malaysia, with statements taken from 15 officers and five civilian witnesses.

items.title: Lodge online campaign material complaints to MCMC - EC
items.summary: PONTIAN: The Election Commission (EC) has called on the public to report online posters or campaign materials unrelated to candidates in the Johor state election directly to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). EC Chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the commission's enforcement team had removed physical campaign materials at certain locations following complaints. "However, campaign materials distributed via online platforms fall under MCMC's jurisdiction.

items.title: Johor polls: EC says online campaign‑material complaints should go directly to MCMC
items.summary: PONTIAN, July 10 — The Election Commission (EC) has called on the public to report online posters or campaign materials unrelated to candidates in the Johor state election directly to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). EC Chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the commission’s enforcement team had removed physical campaign materials at certain locations following complaints. “However, campaign materials distributed via online platforms fall under MCMC’s jurisdiction.

items.title: High stakes in Johor: Why anything less than 40 seats is a setback for BN
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional's (BN) decision to call an early Johor election has raised expectations for the coalition. Universiti Malaya Academy of Malay Studies professor Datuk Dr Awang Azman Awang Pawi said any significant drop below the 40-seat mark could be viewed as a political setback, even if BN retained control of the Johor government. Azman said the campaign had evolved into a contest between BN's promise of continuity and Pakatan Harapan's (PH) bid to persuade voters that Johor's economic growth should deliver more tangible benefits to ordinary households.

items.title: Anwar touched by boy’s plantation journey to meet him in Segamat, gifts bicycle after visit
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today shared the heartwarming story of nine-year-old Muhammad Afif Ikhwan, who travelled through an oil palm plantation with his grandmother in the hope of meeting him. “What may appear to us as a small dream was, in fact, big enough to move his grandmother, Rosimah Mohammad, to ride a motorcycle through an oil palm plantation to fulfil her grandson’s wish. Yesterday, Bernama reported that Rosimah, 58, from Kampung Pudu, Segamat, rode a motorcycle along oil palm plantation tracks solely to give her grandson the opportunity to see the Prime Minister up close.

items.title: PM: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao road link to spur Malaysia-Thailand border economic growth
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM, July 10 — The new road alignment linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah and the Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex in Thailand will serve as a catalyst to propel economic growth in both regions, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the project represented a historic milestone in Malaysia-Thailand relations and would strengthen cross-border connectivity, trade activities and economic cooperation between the two countries. Anwar said the initiative was part of broader efforts to establish a special economic border zone involving northern Malaysia and southern Thailand to spur economic growth and improve the livelihoods of communities in the border areas.

items.title: Chegubard disqualified from Negeri Sembilan polls after RM5,000 fine in sedition case
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 10 — Activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, can no longer contest in the upcoming Negeri Sembilan election following a RM5,000 fine imposed by the Sessions Court today, after he was found guilty of publishing seditious material two years ago. His lawyer, Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, said the defence respected the court’s decision but would be filing an appeal soon, Berita Harian (BH) reported. “However, with this RM5,000 fine, Chegubard is disqualified from contesting.

items.title: Kulasegaran launches Perak Bar Mediation Centre, says disputes can be settled in a day
items.summary: IPOH, July 10 — Long-running and backlogged civil cases pending before the courts can now be referred to mediation service without being withdrawn, allowing disputes to be resolved in as little as one day. Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said mediation services provide faster and more cost-effective way to resolve disputes. Kulasegaran suggested parties involved in pending civil suits do not have to withdraw their cases to pursue mediation, but instead apply to the court for the proceedings to be stayed temporarily and seek the judge’s permission to refer the dispute to mediation.

items.title: Seberang Perai council upgrades 70 playgrounds, plans to revitalise 630 more neighbourhood parks
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, July 10 — The Seberang Perai City Council (MBSP) has upgraded about 70 neighbourhood playgrounds in housing estates over the past three years, with plans to continue upgrading the remaining 630 open spaces. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said MBSP currently manages about 700 open spaces in existing housing estates across Seberang Perai. He said these open spaces were handed over by developers of the housing estates many years ago without playgrounds or recreational amenities.

items.title: Malaysia records over 42,000 dengue cases, 34 deaths this year amid sharp rise in Klang Valley, says MOH
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 10 — The number of dengue cases recorded nationwide this year rose 34.8 per cent to 42,848 as of yesterday, compared with 31,790 cases recorded during the same period last year. Epidemiological analysis showed that nearly two-thirds, or 63.5 per cent, of all dengue cases in Malaysia were reported in Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya. Selangor recorded 19,313 cases, a 43.2 per cent increase from the same period last year, while Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya reported 7,909 cases, up 83.8 per cent.

items.title: Banting teen accused of stabbing classmate faces attempted murder charge, court orders psychiatric evaluation
items.summary: BANTING, July 10 — The Magistrates’ Court here today ordered a Form Three student involved in a stabbing incident at a school in Banting, Kuala Langat, on Monday to be sent to Hospital Bahagia for psychiatric assessment. No plea was recorded from the 15 year old accused when the charge was read out by a court interpreter. Earlier, the teenager arrived at court at 8.33am in a police vehicle.

items.title: MOE outlines data‑driven safety assessment covering attendance, behaviour and CCTV in schools, teacher training institutes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) uses data-driven assessments, monitoring, auditing and compliance with established regulations and procedures to evaluate the actual safety level in schools and teacher education institutes (IPG). The MOE said the School Inspectorate assesses the effectiveness of safety measures in schools based on seven key indicators, including three-year student attendance trends, Sistem Sahsiah Diri Murid data, findings from Student Voice and Teacher Voice questionnaires, and surprise inspections. The MOE said the level of school safety is also assessed annually through the School Safety Rating System (SPKS), which involves a self-assessment covering six main aspects, namely Student Activity Management, Infrastructure Safety Management, Social Management, Crisis and Disaster Management, Risk Management, and Security Guard Service Management.

items.title: Tourism Malaysia launches first VM2026 outreach in Timor‑Leste, eyes stronger ties
items.summary: JAKARTA, July 10 — Tourism Malaysia has launched its first tourism promotion initiative in Timor-Leste as part of its drive to attract more visitors under the Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026) campaign while deepening tourism cooperation. As part of MYEXPO Dili 2026 on July 8 and 9, Tourism Malaysia organised the Visit Malaysia 2026 Destination Seminar and Table Top, bringing together travel agents, tour operators and tourism stakeholders from Timor-Leste and Malaysia to develop travel packages and strengthen business partnerships. Tourism Malaysia Jakarta Director Hairi Mohd Yakzan said the initiative reflected the agency’s commitment to expanding its presence in emerging tourism markets while promoting the VM2026 campaign, themed “Surreal Experiences”.

items.title: Flights grounded: Malaysia Airlines halts Kuala Lumpur–Taipei routes July 11 citing Typhoon Bavi
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 10 — Malaysia Airlines has grounded flights between Kuala Lumpur and Taipei tomorrow as Typhoon Bavi barrels towards Taiwan, scrapping two services and urging passengers to rebook or seek refunds. The airline said services MH366 (Kuala Lumpur–Taipei) and MH367 (Taipei–Kuala Lumpur) scheduled for July 11 have been called off due to adverse weather conditions. “Passenger and crew safety remains our utmost priority,” Malaysia Airlines said in the statement today.
